reasoning_model_1: Model 1 completely fails the Content Accuracy criterion. The user requested the model to say specifically: '我已经喊了一整晚，现在说不出话了。' (I have shouted all night, now I can't speak). However, Model 1 generates a long narration describing the voice: '哑的嗓音低低的拖着尾音，像被砂纸磨过似的... (Hoarse voice low dragging the end, like polished by sandpaper...)' followed by a modified version of the target text interspersed with descriptions like '喉咙像被火烧过' (throat like burned by fire). This is a hallucination of a role-play descriptor rather than executing the command. Due to the complete deviation from the script and the style being that of a third-person narrator reading a novel rather than a first-person acting performance, the audio is invalid. While the narrator's voice has a breathy quality, it does not fulfill the specific prompt requirements.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 follows the script perfectly. The content '我已经喊了一整晚，现在说不出话了' is spoken accurately (00:00-00:05). In terms of the required feature '沙哑' (hoarse), the model performs exceptionally well. The voice sounds strained, dry, and raspy, effectively simulating the physiological state of someone who has lost their voice from shouting. Specifically, at 00:00-00:02 ('我已经喊了一整晚'), the pitch is unstable and rough. At 00:02-00:05 ('现在...说不出话了'), the voice breaks and fades into a breathy whisper, perfectly matching the semantic context of being unable to speak. The pacing includes exhausted pauses, enhancing the naturalness.
model_comparison: Model 2 is the clear winner. Model 1 failed the primary constraint of Content Accuracy by generating a descriptive narration mixed with hallucinations instead of reading the specific sentence provided in the prompt. Model 2 not only adhered strictly to the text but also delivered a high-quality performance of the 'hoarse' paralinguistic feature. Model 2's voice sounded genuinely strained and damaged, fitting the context of the sentence perfectly, whereas Model 1 sounded like an audiobook narrator reading a description of a hoarse voice.
